a260702p10.summary: Google lost its long-running fight against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc's top judges …
a260702p10.title: The European Court of Justice rules that Google's earlier defeat against a €4.1B European Commission penalty over abusing Android market power should stand (Bloomberg)

a260702p11.summary: Amazon is focusing on building chips for its “critical” consumer devices, the company's top hardware executive told CNBC.
a260702p11.title: An interview with Amazon SVP of Devices Panos Panay on designing custom chips for Echo and Fire TV devices, experimenting with AI-enabled gadgets, and more (Arjun Kharpal/CNBC)

a260702p12.summary: Quantum Systems has raised $1.2 billion in a funding round, more than doubling the German drone startup's valuation …
a260702p12.title: Germany-based Quantum Systems, which sells surveillance drones used by Ukraine, raised $1.2B led by Blackstone, Noteus, Airbus, and Advent at an ~$8B valuation (Yazhou Sun/Bloomberg)

a260702p13.summary: Cloudflare gave AI crawlers a September deadline to separate the bots that gather content for search from those that harvest …
a260702p13.title: Cloudflare sets a September 15 deadline for AI companies to differentiate their web crawlers into search, AI training, and AI agents or face being blocked (Samantha Elkins/NBC News)

a260702p14.summary: President Trump said the U.S. chip maker would make a significant donation to a new type of investment account created by the administration.
a260702p14.title: President Trump says Micron will invest $250M in Trump Accounts; Micron says it will come via an employee matching program and a $250 deposit in some US states (New York Times)

a260702p15.summary: A 19-year-old man with dual U.S. and Estonian citizenship was extradited from Finland to Chicago this week to face criminal charges …
a260702p15.title: The US DOJ says Peter Stokes, a 19-year-old dual US-Estonian citizen, was extradited from Finland to face charges of participating in Scattered Spider hacks (Joe Warminsky/The Record)

a260702p16.summary: The German software giant SAP says it is betting that employees can reinvent jobs instead of eliminating them. Experts are divided on whether it will work.
a260702p16.title: German software giant SAP says it is encouraging workers to invent new, more valuable jobs aided by AI, in a bid to avoid layoffs; SAP cut ~10K staff in 2024 (Jim Tankersley/New York Times)

a260702p17.summary: Early adopters of Amazon Leo should temper expectations. … Amazon says it now has enough satellites operating …
a260702p17.title: Amazon says Leo reached 396 satellites in low-Earth orbit following a recent launch, which is "enough to support continuous service across initial latitudes" (Thomas Ricker/The Verge)

a260702p18.summary: President Trump and his family reaped vast financial rewards from a memecoin that generated losses for hundreds of thousands of investors.
a260702p18.title: Filings: President Trump purchased up to $5M each in Broadcom, Meta, Amazon, Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia stocks on July 23, the same day as his AI action plan (New York Times)

a260702p19.summary: The move marks the company's most aggressive push yet into the fast-growing AI-powered coding tool market …
a260702p19.title: Z.ai launches ZCode, an "Agentic Development Environment" optimized for its new GLM-5.2 model; Z.ai's GLM Coding Plan costs from $16.20 to $144 per month (Michael Nuñez/VentureBeat)

a260702p20.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. and its telecom unit will start renting AI computing resources to US companies next fiscal year …
a260702p20.title: SoftBank and its telecom unit launch SB Neo to offer AI chips and cloud services to big companies, aiming to provide 10GW of capacity in the US by 2030 (Min-Jeong Lee/Bloomberg)

a260702p21.summary: The labs develop the technology, but citizens and their elected representatives must make the rules
a260702p21.title: Sam Altman proposes a "US-led international forum" to establish AI standards, provide analysis of capabilities and risks, and make AI available to allies (Sam Altman/Financial Times)

a260702p22.summary: Microsoft is launching a new AI “company.” It won't be a separate legal entity, and most of its 6,000 people already work at Microsoft.
a260702p22.title: Microsoft establishes an organization with 6,000 staff specializing in engineering, corporate training, and management to support businesses with AI deployments (Todd Bishop/GeekWire)

a260702p23.summary: Global venture funding reached a record $510 billion in the first half of 2026, surpassing the $440 billion invested in all of 2025 …
a260702p23.title: Global VC funding hit a record $510B in H1 2026, with OpenAI and Anthropic accounting for $217B, or 43% of the total; in Q2, VCs put $205B into 5K+ startups (Gené Teare/Crunchbase News)

a260702p7.summary: Roughly two-thirds of investors in the president's memecoin are currently in the red — Morten Christensen made a big bet …
a260702p7.title: Nansen: about two-thirds of Trump's memecoin investors are currently in the red, and 85% of World Liberty's $WLFI buyers on the secondary market are underwater (Wall Street Journal)

a260702p8.summary: Nearly six-in-ten U.S. adults support banning anyone under the age of 16 from using social media sites, according to a new Pew Research Center survey.
a260702p8.title: A survey of US adults finds 56% support banning social media for under-16s, while 21% oppose; majorities across demographic and partisan groups support a ban (Pew Research Center)

a260702p9.summary: A Hong Kong fund tied to SK Hynix has grown so large that it's beginning to move the stock it was built to track.
a260702p9.title: A look at the CSOP SK Hynix Leveraged ETF, which trades in Hong Kong and has ballooned into a $13B fund, becoming the world's largest single-stock leveraged ETF (Bloomberg)
